records.ety: [From Albi and Albigeois, a town and its district in the south of France, in which the sect abounded.]

defs: A sect of reformers opposed to the church of Rome in the 12th centuries.
notes: The Albigenses were a branch of the Catharists (the pure). They were exterminated by crusades and the Inquisition. They were distinct from the Waldenses.
